textPlain: About 15 years ago, we had an excellent resource in my town. Its name was Kinko's, and its design was centered around self service with full service options. The floor room comprised of many printer/work stations where a customer could walk in, approach an available station, and commence with all sorts of fun.I made countless posters from PDFs and GIMP projects. My wall displayed a mosaic of laminated posters made at this facility, including several artistic periodic charts, Vim cheatsheats, Linux commands, Python reference charts and even the 10 commandments of logical fallacies.Functional reference art is dear to me. Alas my eyes have degraded so much that a readable, sufficiently sized poster now exceeds my budget. And such self-service resources are now unknown to me. I know many online services exist to accomplish similar things. It doesn't appeal to me though.

Can't have a post about a periodic table with actual elements without referrencig this absolute gem of comedy (the wordpress PDF link in the very first comment below the reddit post deletion image)https://www.reddit.com/r/xkcd/comments/bw88pb/periodic_wall_...
Kinko's I believe was bought out by Fedex. The one near me basically changed names but still offers the same services.
